title: Divide decimals by whole numbers visually
description: Use tenths and hundredths charts and number lines to divide decimals.
topic_path: Divide decimals by whole numbers visually

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the quotient.* 

 [[☃ numeric-input 3]]${}=0.96 \div6$

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
We can divide the $9$ tenths into $6$ groups so that each group gets $1$ tenth.

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 3]]
[[☃ image 4]]
[[☃ image 5]]
[[☃ image 6]]

We will have $3$ tenths and $6$ hundredths remaining, which is the same as $36$ hundredths. We can divide the $36$ hundredths evenly between $6$ groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

Dividing $36$ hundredths between $6$ groups will give each group $6$ hundredths in addition to $1$ tenth.

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 3]]
[[☃ image 4]]
[[☃ image 5]]
[[☃ image 6]]

When we divide $\blueD{0.96}$ into $6$ equal-sized groups, we get $16$ hundredths in each group.

options.alt: A square divided into 100 equal-sized smaller squares, arranged in 10 rows of 10 squares each to represent 100 parts that equal 1 whole. 96 squares are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the quotient.* 

$0.42 \div3=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
We can divide the $4$ tenths into $3$ groups so that each group gets $1$ tenth.


[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 3]]

We will have $1$ tenth and $2$ hundredths remaining, which is the same as $12$ hundredths. We can divide the $12$ hundredths evenly between $3$ groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

Dividing $12$ hundredths between $3$ groups will give each group $4$ hundredths in addition to $1$ tenth.

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 3]]

When we divide $\blueD{0.42}$ into $3$ equal-sized groups, we get $14$ hundredths in each group.

options.alt: A square divided into 100 equal-sized smaller squares, arranged in 10 rows of 10 squares each to represent 100 parts that equal 1 whole. 42 squares are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the quotient.* 

 [[☃ numeric-input 2]] ${}=0.65 \div5$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
We can divide the $6$ tenths into $5$ groups so that each group gets $1$ tenth.


[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 3]]
[[☃ image 4]]
[[☃ image 5]]

We will have $1$ tenth and $5$ hundredths remaining, which is the same as $15$ hundredths. We can divide the $15$ hundredths evenly between $5$ groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

Dividing $15$ hundredths between $5$ groups will give each group $3$ hundredths in addition to $1$ tenth.



[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 3]]
[[☃ image 4]]
[[☃ image 5]]



When we divide $\blueD{0.65}$ into $5$ equal-sized groups, we get $13$ hundredths in each group.

options.alt: A square divided into 100 equal-sized smaller squares, arranged in 10 rows of 10 squares each to represent 100 parts that equal 1 whole. 65 squares are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the quotient.* 

$0.88 \div2=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
We can divide the $8$ tenths into $2$ groups so that each group gets $4$ tenths.

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]

We will have $8$ hundredths remaining. We can divide the $8$ hundredths evenly between $2$ groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

Dividing $8$ hundredths between $2$ groups will give each group $4$ hundredths in addition to $4$ tenths.

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]



When we divide $\blueD{0.88}$ into $2$ equal-sized groups, we get $44$ hundredths in each group.

options.alt: A square divided into 100 equal-sized smaller squares, arranged in 10 rows of 10 squares each to represent 100 parts that equal 1 whole. 88 squares are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the quotient.* 

[[☃ numeric-input 3]] $= 0.5 \div2$ 


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
First, let's divide the grid that shows $\blueD{0.5}$ into hundredths.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{5\text { tenths}}$ is equal to $\blueD{50\text { hundredths}}$:

$\blueD{0.5}=\blueD{0.50}$

We can divide the $50$ hundredths into $2$ groups so that each group gets $25$ hundredths.

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]

When we divide $\blueD{0.5}$ into $2$ equal-sized groups, we get $25$ hundredths in each group.

options.alt:
A square divided into 10 equally-sized columns to show tenths. 5 columns are shaded and 5 columns
are not shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the quotient.* 

$0.6 \div3=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
We can divide the $6$ tenths into $3$ groups so that each group gets $2$ tenths.


[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 3]]
When we divide $\blueD{0.6}$ into $3$ equal-sized groups, we get $2$ tenths in each group.

options.alt: A square is divided into 10 equally-sized columns to show tenths. 6 columns are shaded and 4 columns are not shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the quotient.* 

 [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $=0.4 \div5$


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
First, let's divide the grid that shows $\blueD{0.4}$ into hundredths.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{4\text { tenths}}$ is equal to $\blueD{40\text { hundredths}}$:

$\blueD{0.4}=\blueD{0.40}$

We can divide the $40$ hundredths into $5$ groups so that each group gets $8$ hundredths.

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 2]]
[[☃ image 3]]
[[☃ image 4]]
[[☃ image 5]]



When we divide $\blueD{0.4}$ into $5$ equal-sized groups, we get $8$ hundredths in each group.

options.alt:
A square divided into 10 equally-sized columns to show tenths. 4 columns are shaded and 6 columns
are not shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*You may use the models shown to help find the quotient.* 

 [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $=0.6 \div2$


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Each interval on the number line is $\greenD{0.1}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]



We can count the intervals and see that each of the $\greenD2$ pieces is made up of $3$ intervals, or $0.3$.



[[☃ image 1]]



$0.3=0.6\div2$

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, 5 unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark with a dot at 0.6, 3 unlabeled tick marks, and an ending tick mark at 1. Two equal in length arrows are above the number line. First arrow begins at 0 and ends at the third unlabeled tick mark. Second arrow begins at the third unlabeled tick mark and ends at 0.6.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*Hint: Use the number line below to divide $0.9$ into $3$ equal-sized groups.* 

 [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $=0.9\div3$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Each interval on the number line is $\greenD{0.1}$. 



[[☃ image 1]]




We can count the intervals and see that each of the $\greenD3$ pieces is made up of $3$ intervals, or $0.3$.





[[☃ image 1]]




$0.3=0.9\div3$

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, 8 unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark with a dot at 0.9, and an ending tick mark at 1. Three equal in length arrows are above the number line. First arrow begins at 0 and ends at the third unlabeled tick mark. Second arrow begins at the third unlabeled tick mark and ends at the sixth unlabeled tick mark. Third arrow begins at the sixth unlabeled tick mark and ends at 0.9.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*Hint: Use the number line below to divide $0.8$ into $4$ equal-sized groups.* 

$0.8\div4=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]






[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Each interval on the number line is $\greenD{0.1}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]



We can count the intervals and see that each of the $\greenD4$ pieces is made up of $2$ intervals, or $0.2$.





[[☃ image 1]]

$0.8\div4=0.2$

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, 7 unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark at 0.8, 1 unlabeled tick mark, and an ending tick mark at 1. Four equal in length arrows are above the number line. First arrow begins at 0 and ends at the second unlabeled tick mark. Second arrow begins at the second unlabeled tick mark and ends at the fourth unlabeled tick mark. Third arrow begins at the fourth unlabeled tick mark and ends at the sixth unlabeled tick mark. Fourth arrow begins at the sixth unlabeled tick mark and ends at 0.8.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*Hint: Use the number line below to divide $0.32$ into $4$ equal-sized groups.* 

$0.32\div4=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]

![number line with 4 equal jumps starting at 0 and ending at 0.32](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/833ed0eeff4fac0a864a93a2b4a5ec8b85f673fa)
hints:
Each interval on the number line is $\maroonD{0.01}$. 

![the same number line with the distance between ticks labeled plus 0.01](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b94ba029089ce3b09498bea656a0ae62a4b329e3)

We can count the intervals and see that each of the $4$ pieces is made up of $8$ intervals, or $\greenE{0.08}$.

![the same number line with each jump labeled plus 0.08](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/664d35dbb963b8ef9eb2a12af11c5c54063064de)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*Hint: Use the number line below to divide $0.60$ into $5$ equal-sized groups.* 

[[☃ numeric-input 3]] $=0.60\div 5$

![number line with 5 equal jumps starting at 0 and ending at 0.60](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51ea1a37402e14e0260ea900e8859e6bf489990a)
hints:
Each interval on the number line is $\maroonD{0.01}$. 

![the same number line with the distance between ticks labeled plus 0.01](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b371a2d7123b8b7c640dafc1941a3bfe9e3c0479)

We can count the intervals and see that each of the $5$ pieces is made up of $12$ intervals, or $\greenE{0.12}$.

![the same number line with each jump labeled plus 0.12](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b10ced38c34e551955c5eae9bf44db64e06ee4ce)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*Hint: Use the number line below to divide $0.42$ into $3$ equal-sized groups.* 

$0.42\div 3=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]

![number line with 3 equal jumps starting at 0 and ending at 0.42](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f118f9a762616968892bb876a14aca95dd484398)
hints:
Each interval on the number line is $\maroonD{0.01}$. 

![the same number line with the distance between ticks labeled plus 0.01](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c7e6c43b4fe3cbeee2b8e107e0ce1024243515df)

We can count the intervals and see that each of the $3$ pieces is made up of $14$ intervals, or $\greenE{0.14}$.

![the same number line with each jump labeled plus 0.14](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/732fcc40c8dc1fe3d313a6678cf486f59d9378bc)
